title: Xtra Snappy Kenyan Bean Sprouts Bao
description: A diabetic-friendly Kenyan dish featuring snappy bean sprouts in a long-fermentation-dough bao, packed with umami flavor. This monsoon-inspired recipe is perfect for a healthy and satisfying meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the long-fermentation-dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Steam the bean sprouts until they are tender but still crisp.
Heat the sesame oil in a pan over medium heat and saute the onion, garlic, and ginger until the onion is translucent.
Add the soy sauce, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the steamed bean sprouts to the pan and stir to combine with the sauce.
Place a tablespoon of the bean sprout mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the bao.
Brush the tops of the bao with a little water and sprinkle with chopped scallions.
Bake the bao in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
